title: Saturn's Moon Phoebe: A Cosmic Discovery

story: Music fans rejoiced as The Beatles' Anthology 2 hit stores on March 18, 1996. This treasure trove of rare recordings and outtakes from 1965-68 captivated listeners. The album featured a special treat: 'Real Love', a collaboration blending John Lennon's demo with new performances by the surviving Beatles. Anthology 2 soared to the top of charts in both the US and UK, proving the Fab Four's enduring magic.
activity_question: What special treat did Anthology 2 feature?
bonus_fact: Anthology 2 was part of a three-part series, giving fans a behind-the-scenes look at The Beatles' creative process.

title: A Musical Genius Takes Flight
story: Buzzing melodies filled the air as Nikolai Rimsky-Korsakov entered the world in 1844. Born on March 18 in Tikhvin, Russia, this musical prodigy would grow up to compose incredible pieces like Scheherazade and the famous Flight of the Bumblebee. His lively tunes continue to inspire musicians and delight listeners around the globe.
activity_question: What famous piece did Nikolai Rimsky-Korsakov compose that sounds like an insect?
follow_up_question: Can you hum or whistle a buzzing sound like a bumblebee?

title: A Heart Doctor's Groundbreaking Journey
story: Maude Abbott took her first breath in St Andrews East, Quebec on March 18, 1868. She grew up to become a pioneering Canadian doctor and a world expert on heart problems babies are born with. Abbott's work helped doctors understand these tricky conditions better, paving the way for better care of little patients with heart troubles.
activity_question: What medical field did Maude Abbott become an expert in?
follow_up_question: Why do you think understanding heart problems in babies is important for doctors?

title: A Harlem Renaissance Artist's Colorful Journey
story: Brushstrokes of brilliance began when William H. Johnson entered the world in 1901. Born on March 18 in Florence, South Carolina, Johnson would grow up to become a celebrated African-American artist. His vibrant paintings, like 'Flowers' and 'Training for War', captured the spirit of the Harlem Renaissance. Johnson's art continues to inspire people today, long after his passing in 1970.
activity_question: What type of artist was William H. Johnson?
bonus_fact: The Harlem Renaissance was a cultural movement celebrating African-American art, music, and literature.

title: Jazz Warrior Blows onto the Scene
story: A musical star was born on March 18, 1964. Courtney Pine, destined to become a British jazz sensation, entered the world in London, England. This future saxophone maestro would grow up to dazzle audiences with his smooth sounds and help form the influential Jazz Warriors group. Pine's talent and passion for jazz would inspire a new generation of musicians.
activity_question: What instrument is Courtney Pine known for playing?
